title: Greek Independence Day: A National Day of Celebration of Greek and American Democracy, 2026
summary: Proclamation 11018, issued by the President of the United States on March 24, 2026, declares March 25, 2026, as Greek Independence Day: A National Day of Celebration of Greek and American Democracy. The proclamation commemorates the Greek people's struggle for independence starting in 1821, highlighting their role in renewing the promise of freedom and their historical connection to self-government, which predates the United States. It draws parallels between the founding principles of Greece and the U.S., emphasizing shared values of liberty, self-determination, and republican rule. The text also acknowledges the contributions of the Greek American community to the United States and underscores the strong alliance between Greece and the U.S. in defending Western ideals and promoting peace. The proclamation calls for the observance of this day with appropriate ceremonies and activities, recognizing the enduring spirit of self-governance that inspires both nations.
